notes: v317 — THE BOT NOW TRADES FROM DAY ONE (and the preset chips tell the truth). Follow-up to a tester's 'still getting no buys': his 🧩 combo gate was 📋 adaptive — 'follow whatever MY report card grades SOLID or PROMISING'. Honest maths has a cold-start problem: a young report card has graded NOTHING yet, so that gate closed the pool every single day. And because the 🏆 best-practice button sets that very gate, every fresh install started in a state where the bot could not buy for days. That was a design flaw, not user error — fixed properly. 📋 ADAPTIVE NOW DEGRADES GRACEFULLY: while your card has no graded pairs at all, the gate falls back to the proven starter pairs (🚀 Breakout+🏦 Inst? · 🚀+🔷 Block day · 🚀+🥇 Vol record · 🕵️ Informed?+🏦 Inst?) so the bot trades from its first day — then switches to YOUR card automatically the day any pair grades SOLID/PROMISING with positive edge. Nothing is hidden: the 📜 diary says which path ran (on buys AND on quiet days), and the armed banner shows 'your 📋 report card hasn't graded any pairs yet — the bot uses the proven starter pairs until it does'. Prefer the old wait-for-my-card behaviour? Pick 📋 strict — its label now says 'waits if none' so the trade-off is clear before you choose. PRESET CHIPS FIXED TOO: another tester ran ⚡ Hunter but the ⚖ All-rounder chip stayed highlighted — the gold was hardcoded. The highlight is now computed: whichever rule set exactly matches your saved numbers and gates lights up, hand-edited numbers light nothing, and the caption says 'your own custom mix' so you always know what you're really running. ALSO RECENT — v316 — 'NO SHARES MATCHED' — FOUND THE BUG, AND MADE THE DIARY HONEST. A tester (thanks Tony) reported the bot saying nothing met his criteria no matter the settings. Two things were going on. THE BUG: the bot's automatic once-per-day run could fire the instant fresh data appeared — BEFORE the app had finished preparing streaks, volumes and scores. At that moment the candidate pool is empty by definition, so the bot burned its daily run on an empty scan, wrote 'no shares matched', and refused to try again until tomorrow. Fixed: the automatic run now WAITS until signal preparation is finished, without using up its daily slot — it simply retries in the background every few seconds until the data is ready, then runs once, properly. (The ▶ Run-now button is unchanged — pressing it always runs immediately.) THE HONESTY FIX: when the scanner truly finds nothing, the diary now shows the FUNNEL — how many shares survived each of your rules, in order: 'pool 2,741 → smart list 38 → score≥7 11 → signals 6 → RSI 6 → day-jump 6 → 🟢green 4 → 🧩combo 0'. One glance tells you exactly which rule ate the pool — no more guessing. And when the 📋 adaptive combo gate is the culprit, the diary says why in plain words: your report card has no SOLID or PROMISING pairs yet (tiers firm up as fires build over the first days) and suggests picking a curated pair like 🚀+🏦 or switching the combo gate off while the card warms up. The status banner also now notes when signals are still preparing. A quiet day is still a quiet day — but now the bot can prove it. ALSO RECENT — THE BOT, MADE SIMPLE. Feedback said the Auto-pilot panel was getting confusing — fair call. It now opens with a plain-English STATUS BANNER that answers the only two questions that matter. WHAT DO I DO? A three-step checklist that ticks itself off as you go: ✓ 1. Tap 🏆 and press 💾 Save · ✓ 2. Switch 🤖 ON · ✓ 3. Open the app once a day — it does the rest. WHAT IS IT DOING? The banner tells you outright: when it's off — 'The bot is OFF, three taps and it runs itself every day'; when it's on and waiting — '⏳ ON and armed — the moment today's data finishes loading it will scan, choose by your rules and buy. Nothing to press.'; and after it has run — '✅ Done for today — it bought N shares. It runs again by itself when tomorrow's data loads. Nothing to press.' (Zero buys is explained too: nothing met your rules — that's discipline, not a fault.) And all the expert machinery — scan source, scores, signal ticks, don't-chase gates, 🧩 combo gate, 🎣 dip entry, 🎯 picks, the 📜 decision diary — has folded into one '⚙ Advanced bot settings' section, closed by default and remembered per device. You never need to open it: the 🏆 button already sets everything inside. Open it any time you're curious; close it and the bot is three lines of plain English again. ALSO RECENT — 🏆 THE BEST-PRACTICE BOT, ONE TAP. Everything the last few updates built — the smart exits, the don't-chase gates, the report-card-driven combo gate, the patient dip entry, the separate $50k account — now assembles itself with a single gold button at the top of the 🤖 Auto-pilot panel: '🏆 Set up the best-practice bot'. One tap fills the ENTIRE recommended stack: ⚖ All-rounder risk core (🎯20 🛑8 📦10% 🔢8, 🪤 trail 8, 🛡 break-even +5, ⏱ 10-day time stop) · every don't-chase gate on (🟢 green-day only, skip RSI ≥ 70, skip day-jumps over 8%) · the 🧩 combo gate set to 📋 ADAPTIVE — the bot only buys candidates firing a combination that is currently SOLID or PROMISING with positive edge on YOUR OWN report card, re-checked every data day · 🎣 patient entry at 1.5% under the last close (about a liquid share's median daily dip — tune per share with 📉 Dip Finder) · 🎯 Smart Money source, minimum Watch Score 7, two buys a day, sized by your 📦 rule against the bot's own $50k. Nothing applies until YOU press 💾 Save, and the bot still only starts when YOU switch it ON — the button prepares, you decide. A heads-up worth reading twice: with the adaptive combo gate on, the bot may buy NOTHING for the first days — that is discipline, not failure. It is waiting for your report card to prove a combination is working right now. If you want more action while the card warms up, pick a curated pair (like 🚀 Breakout + 🏦 Inst?) or switch the combo gate off — one dropdown. Practice money only, always. ALSO RECENT — PRE-SET COMBOS FOR THE BOT + THE 📉 DIP FINDER. Two additions straight from the maths. 🧩 COMBO GATE: the Auto-pilot scanner can now demand a COMBINATION before it buys. Pick a curated pair — 🚀 Breakout + 🏦 Inst? (an ignition confirmed by institutional-size volume), 🚀 + 🔷 Block day, 🚀 + 🥇 Vol record, 🕵️ Informed? + 🏦 — or one of the ⛓ SEQUENCES: 🏦 Inst? (or 🕵️) somewhere in the prior 3 days, THEN a 🚀 breakout today — the classic accumulation-then-ignition pattern, checked against each share's own history. Best of all are the 📋 ADAPTIVE options: 'only buy when the candidate is firing a pair that is currently SOLID on MY report card' (or SOLID/PROMISING with positive edge). Here's the honest truth about 'highest success rate': any combo frozen from one good week goes stale — the adaptive gate re-reads YOUR card every data day, so the bot always follows whatever is proving itself NOW. 🎣 PATIENT ENTRY: instead of buying the next day's open, the scanner can place a practice LIMIT buy X% under the last close — you set the depth. And to pick that depth with data instead of a guess, the new 📉 DIP FINDER (button in the Auto-pilot panel) measures any share's REAL behaviour over its last ~80 sessions: the median dip below the previous close, the median high-to-low range, how often a 1% / 2% / 3% / 5% limit would actually fill, and — the part most people never check — the average result 5 days AFTER those fills, measured from your limit price. That last column matters because limit fills cluster on WEAK days (you're buying when sellers are hitting) — deeper limits get better prices but worse company. The Finder ends with a suggested starting depth for that share, because one number never fits all: a 3-cent explorer swings 5–10% a day, a bank barely 1%. Practice money only, as always. ALSO RECENT — THE BOT GETS ITS OWN $50,000 ACCOUNT. 🤖 Auto-pilot now trades a completely SEPARATE practice portfolio, opened with $50,000 — so the robot's results never mix with your own trading, and its Scoreboard becomes a clean experiment. Switch accounts with the new tabs at the top of the 💼 Portfolio window: 👤 MY TRADING (your practice account, exactly as it was) and 🤖 AUTO-PILOT · $50K TEST (the bot's). The ground rules, designed so nothing can cross over by accident: buys you make from the table ALWAYS go to your 👤 account; sells, undo, notes, CSV export and backup-to-file inside the 🤖 view act on the bot's account (so you can step in and close a bot position any time); the bot's 📦 position-sizing and 🔢 limits are measured against ITS $50k, not yours; your 🎯/🛑/🪤/⏱ exit rules manage BOTH accounts on every data load; the 🔔 briefing reports the bot's exits with a 🤖 prefix; the 📊 Scoreboard grades the bot's account only; and the automatic on-device backup now protects both accounts. Notes: any bot trades made BEFORE this update stay in your main history — the $50k account starts clean on purpose, because that's the honest way to test; and 💰 set-starting-balance / ➕ add cash work per-account if you want a different bankroll. Still practice money only, on every account — the bot cannot touch a real broker. ALSO RECENT — THE HONEST-MATHS REBUILD OF THE REPORT CARD. Four upgrades that make the card's verdicts worth trusting — and yes, its numbers WILL look different from last week's card; that's the point. 1) A LONGER MEMORY AND A FAIRER RULER: the card now grades up to ~60 trading days (was 15), and every fire is scored against ITS OWN DAY's all-share average — the 'EDGE'. A strong market week used to flatter every flag; now a flag only scores for what it added beyond its day. 2) CONFIDENCE TIERS ON EVERY LINE: SOLID (many fires, consistent edge), PROMISING (decent evidence), TOO EARLY (could still be luck) — plus '✓ held up in both halves' (the edge appeared in BOTH halves of the window, not one lucky fortnight), the MEDIAN move (so one lottery-ticket micro-cap can't carry a grade), and average wins vs losses. 3) TRADEABLE TRUTH: the graded universe now excludes shares under $50k traded per day — an edge that only exists on stocks you couldn't actually buy is a phantom (the card shows how many were excluded) — and every line shows its edge split by tape: 🟩 risk-on days vs 🟥 risk-off days, because momentum patterns behave differently in each. (Older notes are in the Owner’s Manual changelog.)
